reasoning_model_1: The user command was to read a specific sentence ('Please listen carefully, this step is very important.') with a slow pace. Model 1 failed significantly in two aspects. First, regarding **Content Accuracy**, it suffered from instruction leakage, reading the prompt instructions ('Please read the sentence with a slow pace') aloud rather than just the target text. The content spoken was 'Please read the sentence with a slow pace, please listen carefully this step is very important.' Second, regarding the **Paralinguistic Compliance (Pace)**, the audio is actually fast and hurried (approx 160-180 wpm), completely failing to execute the 'slow pace' requirement. The delivery is energetic and rushed (00:00-00:05), which contradicts the 'important' and 'careful' tone implied by the text and the explicit speed instruction. Due to the failure to isolate the target text and the complete absence of the required feature, this is scored as 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 correctly understood the instruction. regarding **Content Accuracy**, it filtered out the command and only synthesized the target text: 'Please listen carefully, this step is very important.' regarding **Paralinguistic Compliance (Pace)**, the model executed the 'slow pace' directive perfectly. The speech covers the short sentence over approximately 6 seconds (00:00-00:06). There is a distinct, deliberate pause after 'carefully' (around 00:02), and the articulation of words like 'step' and 'important' is elongated and measured. The prosody conveys gravity and clarity, matching the semantic content and the style constraint. It is a high-quality generation.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed on fundamental instruction following by reading the prompt instructions aloud (prompt leakage) and also failed the specific style constraint, generating fast speech instead of slow speech. Model 2 correctly identified the text to be spoken and applied the 'slow pace' feature effectively with natural pauses and elongated articulation. Model 1 receives the lowest score due to the combination of content error and feature failure.
